title: API Reference v3
text:
# API Reference v3.2

Base URL: https://api.acmecloud.com/v3

## Authentication

All requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Tokens expire after 24 hours. Use the /auth/token endpoint to generate a new token.

## Rate Limits

Free tier: 100 requests per minute. Pro tier: 1,000 requests per minute. Enterprise: 10,000 requests per minute. Rate limit headers are included in every response.

## Endpoints

### GET /users
Returns a paginated list of users. Default page size is 25. Maximum page size is 100.

### POST /users
Creates a new user. Required fields: email, name. Optional fields: role, department, phone.

### GET /users/{id}
Returns a single user by ID. Returns 404 if user not found.

### DELETE /users/{id}
Soft-deletes a user. Requires admin role. Deleted users can be restored within 30 days.

title: API Reference v4
text:
# API Reference v4.0

Base URL: https://api.acmecloud.com/v4

## Authentication

All requests require OAuth2 authentication. Bearer tokens are issued via the /oauth/token endpoint. Tokens expire after 1 hour. Refresh tokens are valid for 30 days.

## Rate Limits

Free tier: 200 requests per minute. Pro tier: 2,000 requests per minute. Enterprise: unlimited. Rate limit information is available via the /rate-limit endpoint.

## Endpoints

### GET /users
Returns a paginated list of users. Default page size is 50. Maximum page size is 200.

### POST /users
Creates a new user. Required fields: email, full_name, role. Optional fields: department, phone, timezone.

### GET /users/{id}
Returns a single user by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

### DELETE /users/{id}
Permanently deletes a user and all associated data. Requires admin role. This action cannot be undone.

title: Getting Started Guide
text:
# Getting Started with AcmeCloud

## Prerequisites

You need Node.js 16 or higher and npm 8 or higher. Python users should have Python 3.8 or higher installed.

## Installation

Install the SDK using npm:

npm install @acmecloud/sdk

Or using pip:

pip install acmecloud

## Quick Start

First, generate an API key from the dashboard at https://dashboard.acmecloud.com/settings/api-keys.

Then initialize the client:

const client = new AcmeCloud({ apiKey: 'your-key-here' });

## Authentication

All API requests use Bearer token authentication. Generate a token by calling the /auth/token endpoint with your API key. Tokens are valid for 24 hours.

## Support

For help, email support@acmecloud.com or visit our community forum at https://community.acmecloud.com.

title: Quickstart Guide
text:
# AcmeCloud Quickstart

## Requirements

Node.js version 16+ and npm 8+ are required. For Python, use Python 3.8 or later.

## Install

Using npm:

npm install @acmecloud/sdk

Using pip:

pip install acmecloud

## Setup

Get your API key from the settings page at https://dashboard.acmecloud.com/settings/api-keys.

Initialize the SDK:

const acme = new AcmeCloud({ apiKey: 'YOUR_API_KEY' });

## Authentication

The SDK handles authentication automatically using your API key. Under the hood, it uses Bearer tokens that expire after 24 hours.

## Need Help?

Reach out to support@acmecloud.com or check the community forum at https://community.acmecloud.com.

title: Deployment Guide
text:
# Deployment Guide

Last updated: September 2023

## Server Requirements

Minimum: 2 CPU cores, 4GB RAM, 20GB disk. Recommended: 4 CPU cores, 8GB RAM, 50GB disk. AcmeCloud currently supports deployment on AWS and GCP only.

## Deployment Steps

1. Pull the latest Docker image: docker pull acmecloud/server:latest
2. Configure environment variables (see .env.example)
3. Run database migrations: acme migrate up
4. Start the server: docker compose up -d

## SSL Configuration

SSL certificates must be manually configured using Let's Encrypt. Automatic SSL is not supported at this time.

## Monitoring

Use the built-in /health endpoint for basic health checks. For detailed monitoring, integrate with Datadog or New Relic using our monitoring plugin. Contact devops@acmecloud.com for setup assistance.

title: Deployment Guide (Current)
text:
# Deployment Guide

Last updated: January 2026

## Server Requirements

Minimum: 2 CPU cores, 8GB RAM, 40GB disk. Recommended: 8 CPU cores, 16GB RAM, 100GB disk. AcmeCloud supports deployment on AWS, GCP, Azure, and self-hosted Kubernetes.

## Deployment Steps

1. Install the AcmeCloud CLI: npm install -g @acmecloud/cli
2. Run: acme deploy init
3. Follow the interactive setup wizard
4. Deploy: acme deploy push

## SSL Configuration

SSL is automatically provisioned and renewed through our built-in certificate manager. No manual configuration required.

## Monitoring

Built-in observability dashboard available at /admin/monitoring. Supports OpenTelemetry for integration with Datadog, Grafana, New Relic, and other observability platforms.

title: Changelog
text:
# Changelog

## v4.0.0 (2025-12-01)

- Breaking: Authentication switched from API key + Bearer token to OAuth2
- Breaking: DELETE /users now permanently deletes instead of soft-delete
- New: Added /oauth/token endpoint
- New: Added timezone field to user profiles
- Changed: Default page size increased from 25 to 50
- Changed: Maximum page size increased from 100 to 200
- Removed: /auth/token endpoint (replaced by /oauth/token)

## v3.2.1 (2025-06-15)

- Fixed: Rate limit headers missing on 429 responses
- Fixed: Pagination returning duplicate results

## v3.2.0 (2025-03-01)

- New: Added rate limit headers to all responses
- New: Added /rate-limit endpoint for checking current usage
- Changed: Free tier rate limit increased from 60 to 100 requests per minute

title: Security Best Practices
text:
# Security Best Practices

## API Key Management

Store API keys in environment variables, never in source code. Rotate keys every 90 days. Use separate keys for development and production.

## Token Handling

Bearer tokens expire after 24 hours. Store tokens securely and refresh them before expiry. Never log tokens in application logs.

## Data Encryption

All data is encrypted at rest using AES-256. Data in transit is protected by TLS 1.2. Client-side encryption is available for Enterprise customers.

## Access Control

Use the principle of least privilege. Assign roles based on job function. Regular access reviews should be conducted quarterly. Admin access requires multi-factor authentication.

title: Troubleshooting Guide
text:
# Troubleshooting

## Common Issues

### 401 Unauthorized

Your API key or token is invalid or expired. Generate a new token using the /auth/token endpoint. Make sure you're including the Bearer prefix in the Authorization header.

### 429 Too Many Requests

You've exceeded your rate limit. Free tier allows 100 requests per minute. Upgrade to Pro for 1,000 requests per minute. Check the X-RateLimit-Remaining header to monitor usage.

### 500 Internal Server Error

This is a server-side issue. Check the status page at https://status.acmecloud.com. If the issue persists, contact support@acmecloud.com with your request ID from the X-Request-Id header.

### Connection Timeout

Ensure your firewall allows outbound connections to api.acmecloud.com on port 443. If using a proxy, configure the SDK with your proxy settings.
